description: Core eval cho research agent: routing đúng tool + đúng args, hỏi lại khi thiếu (ask_user), xác nhận trước khi gửi, và xử lý câu ngoài phạm vi / không cần tool. Chấm trên tool_calls + args (subset). KHÔNG sửa file này — chỉ thêm case vào eval_group.json.

items.title: Alors, je vais t’expliquer comment je fais L’image est en japonais, mais la procédure est probablement la même.
items.summary:
Alors, je vais t’expliquer comment je fais L’image est en japonais, mais la procédure est probablement la même.
Je suis sur Android avec Chrome.

1.2. Va sur la page de création de bot
3. Nom du bot
4. Description du bot
5. Modèle du bot → tu peux choisir GPT-4o (version 2024) ici
6. Écris le comportement et la personnalité du bot
7. Dans « Visibilité », choisis « Only you (moi uniquement) »  ※ Le point 7 est vraiment important, n’oublie pas !Ensuite, appuie sur le bouton « Publish ».  Ça devrait créer ton bot.

Dans les réglages avancés, tu peux aussi ajuster la température et d’autres détails, donc regarde si tu veux affiner plus tard. Il y a peut-être d’autres façons de faire, mais moi je crée mes chatbots comme ça. 
#Poe #AI

items.title: Stock Market Today: New Nvidia Chip Fuels AI Enthusiasm, Oil Climbs After Mideast Hostilities — Live Updates - WSJ
items.summary: # Stock Market Today: Dow Opens Lower, Oil Climbs; New Nvidia Chip Fuels AI Enthusiasm — Live Updates. S&P 500 7580.15 0.00%. Nasdaq 26999.93 0.10%. U.S. 10 Yr -27/32 4.501%. KBW Nasdaq Bank Index 168.21 -0.72%. S&P GSCI Index Spot 713.01 2.65%. # Stock Market Today: New Nvidia Chip Fuels AI Enthusiasm. U.S. stock futures rose this morning, driven by enthusiasm around AI, though they were later knocked by Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency that the country was suspending negotiations with the U.S. over Israel's conduct. Adding to the AI fervor this morning was Nvidia’s unveiling of its new superchip, which it described as “the most efficient PC chip ever built.” The world’s most valuable company will work with manufacturers including Dell Technologies, Microsoft and Lenovo to build laptops, which will be designed for running AI agents. South Korea’s Kospi index, which is dominated by AI chip makers Samsung and SK Hynix, surged 3.7%, bringing its year-to-date gains to 109%.

items.title: NEC Wins at the Newsweek AI Impact Awards 2026: Press Releases - nec.com
items.summary: - NEC recognized for its Management Dashboard initiative targeted at accelerating decision-making with AI -. **Tokyo, June 2, 2026 -** NEC Corporation (NEC; TSE: 6701) today announced that it has been named a winner at the Newsweek AI Impact Awards 2026 (\*1). NEC was recognized for its Management Dashboard initiative targeted at accelerating decision-making with AI in the category of AI Workplace: Best Outcomes, Leadership and Succession Planning. dotData is an AI-powered data analytics platform (\*2) that automates the entire data science process and provides a one-stop solution for AI development, from extracting features from business data to building predictive models using machine learning. Additionally, in its shift from a traditional systems integrator to a "Value Driver," NEC restructured its value creation model under the name "NEC BluStellar" (\*5), which leverages NEC's cutting-edge technologies, developed and refined through years of experience and proven cross-industry expertise, aiming to transform business models, address social challenges, resolve management issues faced by customers, and lead them into a brighter future.

items.title: This AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies - TechCrunch
items.summary: A new AI weather forecasting tool released today by the startup WindBorne Systems offers more frequent and accurate predictions on key variables than the world-leading system developed by European governments, thanks to advancements in how sensor readings are fed into deep learning models. WindBorne says the new version of its model offers a more accurate forecast than the ECMWF’s traditional and AI systems across several variables. Today marks the release of the sixth version of that model, WeatherMesh, which the company says is more accurate than traditional and AI forecasts produced by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasting (ECMWF), the European intergovernmental organization seen by meteorologists as the leading provider of accurate weather prediction today. But WindBorne and other organizations are working to feed data directly into the models, and the company’s head of AI, Joan Creus-Costa, says the direct ingestion of data from their balloons and other sources is the key reason for improvement in the new version of WeatherMesh.

items.title: AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan - The Washington Post
items.summary: AI & Tech Brief from WP Intelligence. # AI & Tech Brief: Exclusive | AI ad campaigns slam Trahan. Plus, Anthropic files for a blockbuster initial public offering. Make us preferred on Google. Sign up here to get this newsletter in your inbox. * **Exclusive:** AI safety group Americans for Responsible Innovation rolls out a six-figure ad campaign this week attacking Rep. Lori Trahan (D-Massachusetts), arguing that she’s “taking the side” of AI oligarchs in her bipartisan legislative efforts. * The AI safety community doesn’t want to hand power of AI regulation to President Donald Trump’s administration now that states are passing more rigorous AI laws — and the California and New York attorneys general are challenging big AI companies. * Nvidia is rolling out a new “world model” simulator for robots and a new design platform for robot developers. In doing so, the company is running the same business playbook it did to become so dominant in the chip market.

items.title: Elementos secures new Andalusian tenements - Mining.com.au
items.summary: The new tenure positions Elementos as the dominant landholder in the region (Andalucía, Spain), with the company considering project expansion possibilities for tin and other critical minerals around the Oropesa Project. The new tenements — Laurencia, Pascuala, and San Jose — provide Elementos with immediate along-strike proximity to the already defined tin and zinc mineral resources and tin reserve. *“These tenements provide a pathway to resource additional tin and EU critical minerals with the potential to extend the project life and/or create new standalone projects for the benefit of our shareholders and the local communities within which we operate.”*. Laurencia and Pascuala are located along strike to the west of the existing Oropesa Investigation Permit and within the same geological sequence of the Oropesa tin and zinc resources. Elementos is a tin and critical minerals development company focused on becoming a key supplier to global technology and electrification markets, with its flagship asset being the Oropesa Tin Project in Spain.

items.title: Elementos brings general manager on board for Spain operations - Mining.com.au
items.summary: Elementos (ASX:ELT) has appointed Antonio Martín Sánchez as the company’s General Manager for its Spain-based operations, effective 1 July 2026. This will include the development of the Oropesa Tin Project in Andalucía and the project’s alignment with the Robledollano Tin Smelter, in which the company has a binding option to acquire a 50% interest stake. He has contributed to delivering reduced emissions, energy efficiency, and environmental, social, and governance (ESG) reporting in previous roles, which Elementos says aligns with its commitment to responsible development and its ambition to establish a *“modern, responsible, and reliable tin supply chain”*from Spain for Europe. *“The board welcomes Antonio to MESPA and looks forward to his contribution as we progress the growth of our vertically integrated tin operations.”*. Elementos is a tin and critical minerals development company focused on becoming a key supplier to global technology and electrification markets, with its flagship asset being the Oropesa Tin Project in Spain.

items.title: Column: Tin re-balances but investors are still betting on scarcity - Mining.com
items.summary: Silver Futures $ 75.495 / ozt  7.47%. Micro Gold Futures $ 4713.1 / ozt  3.80%. Micro Silver Futures $ 75.48 / ozt  7.54%. Gold Futures $ 4713.3 / ozt  3.84%. LME three-month tin is trading at $55,225 per metric ton, close to the all-time high of $59,040 reached during the frenzied cross-metals rally in January. The London tin price is up by 36% since the start of the year. The next best performer is aluminum, which has risen by 23% and is experiencing an unprecedented supply shock after the loss of Gulf production due to the Iran war. Tin supply has no direct exposure to the war in the Gulf, unlike aluminum, nickel and copper. Global mine production is on course to grow by a healthy 8.7% this year, according to the International Tin Association (ITA). Production at the Bisie mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo is stable after last year’s brief closure due to encroaching M23 insurgents.

items.title: Lead-Free Perovskites Push Solar Cells Toward Safer High-Efficiency Designs - AZoM
items.summary: **By replacing toxic lead with safer alternatives such as tin, bismuth, antimony, and double perovskites, researchers are charting a path toward next-generation solar cells that could combine high performance with improved environmental safety.**. Image 4: Figure oulined the schematic representation of lead-free perovskite structure (A site represents monovalent cation, X represents a halide anion with bi/trivalent cation from each type of perovskite family) and the year-wise progression of the most efficient PSCs from each lead-free perovskite family from 2016 to 2025. _Figure oulined the schematic representation of lead-free perovskite structure (A site represents monovalent cation, X represents a halide anion with bi/trivalent cation from each type of perovskite family) and the year-wise progression of the most efficient PSCs from each lead-free perovskite family from 2016 to 2025._. The review highlighted key design approaches that have helped raise tin-based lead-free device power conversion efficiencies from below 1% to 17.13%, thereby providing a research roadmap for developing more scalable, high-performance, and environmentally sustainable solar technologies.

items.title: Strategic development of stable and efficient lead-free perovskite solar cells - Nature
items.summary: # Strategic development of stable and efficient lead-free perovskite solar cells. Lead-free perovskite solar cells have seen rapid growth in recent years and pose a strong contender for future photovoltaic technology due to their non-toxic behavior. However, lead-free perovskite solar cells suffer from stability issues and low efficiency. In 2025, Tin-based perovskite solar cells achieved a maximum device efficiency of 17.13%. In this review, we discussed various strategies to develop stable and efficient lead-free perovskite solar cells. A brief discussion on the lead-free perovskite solar cells as alternatives and their fundamental issue has been presented. Importantly, recent developments in lead-free perovskite solar cells have been presented to emphasize the trends and techniques for highly efficient materials. By altering the A, B, or X site in perovskites, their features, such as bandgap, charge transport, optical absorption, and stability, can be tuned to make them highly attractive for solar energy applications22.").

items.title: Union on a hot tin roof - Research Professional News
items.summary: Research Professional News   Published by Clarivate. # Union on a hot tin roof. **The sun is out at the University and College Union’s annual congress in Harrogate**. University and College Union representatives visiting Yorkshire this week might not have time to visit Harrogate’s famous Turkish baths or the original branch of Bettys Tea Rooms, but we can think of worse places to spend the next few days. Amid soaring temperatures, the union will debate its policy priorities at the Harrogate Convention Centre until Friday. What’s on the bill? Well, the UCU will need to be out by Friday night for the Taylor Swift tribute act in the Royal Hall, but perhaps we should focus on what happens before then. ### Sign in to your account. items.title: 🚨 the richest man on the planet just lost a $134 billion lawsuit in under two hours..
items.summary:
🚨 the richest man on the planet just lost a $134 billion lawsuit in under two hours..

Elon Musk sued OpenAI, Sam Altman, and Microsoft.. claimed they stole a nonprofit he helped build and turned it into a money machine.. wanted Altman fired, the company dismantled, and $134 billion returned

the jury didn't even get to whether OpenAI did anything wrong.. they said Musk filed too late.. statute of limitations.. three years to sue and he missed the window

think about that.. a man who runs X, advises the president, launches rockets, and builds AI at xAI.. couldn't beat a filing deadline

in 2015 Musk put $38 million into OpenAI.. in 2018 he walked away.. in 2023 he built xAI to compete with them.. in 2024 he sued.. in 2026 a jury said you should've spoken up sooner

two billionaires went to war over who gets to control the technology that might replace the rest of us.. and it ended not with a verdict on right or wrong.. but on a calendar

items.title: everyone's paying openai $200/mo. i paid nvidia $2,999 once
items.summary:
everyone's paying openai $200/mo. i paid nvidia $2,999 once

2 months later i'm up $22,000 net

was building a micro-SaaS last months. inference costs eating 35% of revenue

scaling users meant scaling losses. something had to change

bought a local AI box in january, moved everything on-device

API bill: $340/mo -> $11 in electricity

then clients started asking: "does my data go to openai?"

when the answer was no - they didn't just stay. they paid more

raised pricing 2.7x for a "fully private" tier

same model, same outputs, just running on hardware i own

8 clients paying $149/mo for on-premise AI now

4 poached from competitors who couldn't offer it

few months breakdown:
> box: $2,999 one-time
> electricity: $100
> API costs avoided: $4,080
> new revenue from private tier: $21,400

box paid for itself in month 8. every dollar after is 99% margin

Bookmark it asap and use before all noticed

most people obsess over prompts. nobody optimizes infrastructure

cloud isn't cheaper. it's someone else's profit margin disguised as convenience

items.title: 🚨 do you understand what is about to happen to the stock market..
items.summary:
🚨 do you understand what is about to happen to the stock market..

Three of the most valuable private companies on earth - SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic - are all racing to go public in the same year. 

Together they could dump close to $3 trillion of market value onto public investors in a matter of months.

- Anthropic, the company behind Claude, went from $9B to $30B+ annualized revenue in 15 months - roughly 1,400% growth
- It's now valued around $965B, passing OpenAI for the first time ever
- 2026's IPO lineup is unreal: SpaceX (up to $75B raise, biggest in history), OpenAI targeting fall, and Anthropic eyeing October
- Big funds are already hoarding cash and may sell Alphabet and other megacaps just to make room

The AI gold rush is no longer private. The shovel sellers are about to ring the bell.

items.title: 🚨 THIS IS NOT LOOKING GOOD
items.summary:
🚨 THIS IS NOT LOOKING GOOD

SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic will go public at the same time.

That will force the market to absorb $200 BILLION of new supply.

When that happens, funds don't find new money out of thin air.

They sell what has already gone up.

NVIDIA, SK HYNIX, Micron, INTEL: those are the bags that will get cut first.

And if the leaders dump, the S&P 500 dumps with them.

We saw the same pattern after COVID.

Hype IPOs flooded the market --> liquidity got tighter --> air came out fast

This time, the AI bottleneck trade looks even more crowded.

Watch the upcoming IPOs closely.

That's where you may first see what the market is forced to sell.

they just filed to go public.

the draft S-1 hit the SEC today. the most anticipated AI IPO is officially in motion.

let's put this in perspective:
→ days ago they hit a $965B valuation
→ that's nearly triple their February number ($380B)
→ they already passed OpenAI in value and revenue
→ $47B annual revenue, up from $9B a year ago
→ now they're taking it public

here's the part that makes it wild:

5 years ago Anthropic didn't exist.

Dario Amodei left OpenAI in 2021. took his sister Daniela and a handful of researchers. no product. no revenue. just a bet on building AI safely.

today that company is filing for one of the biggest tech IPOs in history.

items.title: EVERYONE'S CALLING IT COMMUNISM
items.summary:
EVERYONE'S CALLING IT COMMUNISM

Bernie Sanders just proposed a one-time 50% tax on OpenAI, Anthropic and xAI - paid in stock, not cash - dropping half of each company into a public fund that pays dividends to every American.

The catch nobody in the replies noticed: the AI bosses asked for this first.

- OpenAI literally proposed "a public wealth fund giving every citizen a stake in AI-driven growth"
- Anthropic publicly called for "national sovereign wealth funds with stakes in AI"
- Elon Musk said federal checks were "the best way to deal with unemployment caused by AI"
- Trump's government already took a 10% stake in Intel and a 15% cut of Nvidia's China chip sales - nobody screamed socialism

So the plan everyone's calling a commie fever dream was pitched by Altman, Amodei and Musk before Bernie ever said a word.

args.text:
Tôi là một trợ lý nghiên cứu (research assistant) có khả năng truy cập các công cụ để hỗ trợ bạn. Tôi có thể:

1. **Tìm kiếm & tra cứu**: Tìm thông tin trên internet, bài báo khoa học (arXiv), mạng xã hội, hoặc tài liệu nội bộ.
2. **Đọc nội dung**: Trích xuất văn bản từ các trang web, bài báo, tweet/post.
3. **Tổng hợp & định dạng**: Biên tập thông tin thành các dạng bản tin, danh sách bullet, thread hoặc bài viết có cấu trúc.
4. **Hỗ trợ quyết định**: Tra cứu chính sách, quy định nội bộ nếu cần.
5. **Chủ động & nhanh**: Tôi cố gắng hoàn thành yêu cầu trong một bước, tự đưa ra giả định hợp lý thay vì hỏi lại bạn khi thiếu thông tin.

Bạn cứ yêu cầu — tôi sẽ xử lý ngay!

items.title: OpenAI Robotics is hiring, looking for exceptional full-stack hardware, ops, systems, and ML engineers to help us progra
items.summary:
OpenAI Robotics is hiring, looking for exceptional full-stack hardware, ops, systems, and ML engineers to help us program and manufacture robots that are useful for society.

AI should be able to help people in the physical world. In the short term, we are focused on robots to support skilled workers to build our future infrastructure; in the long term, we imagine everyone having a personal robot doing anything they need.

Our world simulation research program, led by Aditya Ramesh (@model_mechanic), has evolved over the past year into OpenAI Robotics. Progress is rapid, and based on a foundation of co-design between robotics hardware and ML research.

If you love working hands-on across the robotics stack and want to build the future, please consider joining us. Send an email with your background and evidence of exceptional accomplishment to: robotics-recruiting@openai.com

Grokstream says the next generation of IT operations will not be built around more dashboards, more rules, or faster alert routing. In this Technology Reseller News podcast, Doug Green speaks with Josh Kindiger, Co-Founder and COO of Grokstream, about how the company is helping MSPs, CSPs and enterprise IT organizations move from reactive operations toward predictive, self-healing IT environments. Grokstream is positioning Grok as that layer: a predictive and agentic AI platform that helps operations teams reduce noise, prevent incidents, improve engineer experience and move toward self-healing IT operations. * ***Grokstream Announces Grok® L1 Agent to Advance Predictive and Agentic AI for IT Operations***.

items.title: The laptop hasn't changed in 30 years. NVIDIA just changed it
items.summary:
The laptop hasn't changed in 30 years. NVIDIA just changed it

RTX Spark is their first PC chip ever.

- RTX 5070 level GPU
- 128GB unified memory
- 1 petaflop of local AI
- thin, light, barely throttles unplugged

Your AI agent lives on the machine. 24/7. No cloud.

This is step one of the agentic AI PC, and everyone else is about to copy it.

items.title: 🚨do you understand what just happened with NVIDIA RTX Spark..
items.summary:
🚨do you understand what just happened with NVIDIA RTX Spark..

Jensen Huang walked on stage and pulled an entire gaming PC out of his pocket. 

NVIDIA merged the CPU, RTX GPU, AI hardware and up to 128GB of memory into one Windows-on-ARM superchip and called it the end of the PC as you know it.

> 20-core Grace CPU plus a Blackwell GPU with 6,144 CUDA cores - RTX 5070-tier graphics in a 14mm body.
> NVIDIA claims 100+ FPS at 1440p in 007 First Light and Forza Horizon 6, on battery.
> It runs a 120-billion-parameter AI model locally, no cloud needed.
> ASUS, Dell, HP, Lenovo, MSI and Microsoft already have 30+ laptops lined up for this fall.

The whole internet has two questions: is it real, and how much. Nobody's asking the third - what happens to every other chipmaker if it is.

items.title: Windows users, this one’s for you.
items.summary:
Windows users, this one’s for you.

Computer use now works on Windows, so Codex can take action on your Windows computer.

And with Windows support for Codex in the ChatGPT mobile app, you can start, review, and steer tasks on the go while work continues on your Windows machine.

An early experience, but we’re working on more ways to keep your work moving, wherever you are.

items.title: How we prompt AI is very different in 2026 than 2022 when ChatGPT came out.
items.summary:
How we prompt AI is very different in 2026 than 2022 when ChatGPT came out.

I'm teaching a new course, AI Prompting for Everyone, to help you become an AI power user — whatever your current skill level.

It covers skills that apply across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and other AI tools. How to use deep research mode for well-researched reports on complex questions. How to give AI the right context, including more documents and images than most people realize you can provide. When to ask AI to think hard for several minutes on important decisions like what car to buy, what to study, or what job to take. And how to use AI to generate images, analyze data, and build simple games and websites.

I also cover intuitions about how these models work under the hood, so you know when to trust an answer and when not to.

Along the way, you'll see flying squirrels, a creativity test, some of my old family photos, and fireworks.

Join me at https://t.co/tcQc4iJAJG

items.title: Tesla down 3.5% today, there are several news stories working against it :
items.summary:
Tesla down 3.5% today, there are several news stories working against it :

-OpenAI entering the robotics space: 
Sam Altman/OpenAI announced hiring for a robotics team, seen as direct competition to Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot project. This has weighed on sentiment around Tesla's AI/robotics growth narrative.

Legal/regulatory issues in China: A consumer fraud lawsuit over Full Self-Driving (FSD) marketing went to its first hearing. Owners claim Tesla overstated capabilities (no full regulatory approval in China), seeking damages. Tesla has reportedly rebranded FSD as "Tesla Assisted Driving" there. China remains a key market, so this adds pressure.

The Space X IPO still looms and a narrative of - will Tesla shareholders sell to participate in it? I don't think it will make a meaningful difference to Tesla stock and will likely run with it as part of the Musk empire.

items.title: Two big pieces of news that are very bullish for Kraken Robotics ( $KRKNF $PNG.V) dropped last month. Both about Anduril
items.summary:
Two big pieces of news that are very bullish for Kraken Robotics ( $KRKNF $PNG.V) dropped last month. Both about Anduril's Dive-XL.

Kraken supplies Anduril's Dive-XL and makes more than CAD $10M from every single one.

1) Today: AUKUS announced its first signature Pillar 2 project, focused on underwater drones. UK alone committed >£150M. Anduril publicly confirmed it's involved.

2) Military Times Dive-XL feature (May 11). Anduril Maritime's Head of Growth on the US Navy CAMP program: "in partnership with the US Navy, hopefully move into high rate production here by the end of calendar year." DIU and the US Navy picked Dive-XL for CAMP in March. High-rate production targeted end of CY 2026.

Demand stack:
→ A$1.7B Royal Australian Navy contract
→ US Navy CAMP, high-rate production targeted end of CY 2026
→ AUKUS Pillar 2 underwater drones project, Anduril publicly involved

Anduril is private at ~US$30B+. Kraken is the public way to own the supplier base.

items.title: OpenAI heralds cybersecurity, election interference safeguard plans for 2026 midterms - CyberScoop
items.summary: The announcement builds on work from major tech firms in 2024 to combat AI-infused election chicanery. OpenAI on Wednesday hailed its plans to safeguard information and aid cybersecurity defenders in the 2026 midterm elections, including work to combat deepfakes and other forms of artificial intelligence misuse. The announcement builds on commitments from major tech companies in 2024, including OpenAI, to protect elections from AI-infused election interference — efforts that some thought weren’t enough. OpenAI highlighted that it has made its Codex Security agentic framework and Trusted Access for Cyber framework available to election officials, and was briefing the National Association of Secretaries of State and the National Association of State Election Directors on its tools. One new element of Wednesday’s announcement is that OpenAI has struck a partnership with the Associated Press on sharing election data.

items.title: OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.' - GIGAZINE
items.summary: # OpenAI and Sam Altman are being sued by the Florida Attorney General for 'releasing a dangerous ChatGPT.'. James Ousmayer, Attorney General of Florida, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, alleging deceptive practices and damages. **Attorney General James Uthmeier Files First-in-the-Nation State-Led Lawsuit Against OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman for Deceptive Practices and Harms to Floridians | My Florida Legal**. **OpenAI Sued by Florida's Attorney General Over AI Harms - WSJ**. On June 1, 2026, local time, Attorney General James Usmayer filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging that the company deliberately released ChatGPT to the public, including children, while concealing significant risks, suppressing internal safety warnings, and deceiving Florida residents about the true nature and dangers of the product. Attorney General Ousmayer explained, 'Today we announced the first state-led lawsuit in the nation against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman. > Today, we announced the first-in-the-nation state-led lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman.

items.title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, accusing them of putting profit over safety - NBC News
items.summary: # Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, accusing them of putting profit over safety. Florida seeks to hold Sam Altman personally liable, accusing OpenAI of marketing ChatGPT as safe while concealing that it could drive users toward harm. Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier sued OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman on Monday, accusing the company of putting profit over safety, fueling violence and pushing a product it knew could harm users. “The rise of OpenAI is attributable to a web of deceit and the exploitation of users (including Floridians), leveraging their data and safety to boost OpenAI’s market value at unacceptable costs,” the complaint reviewed by NBC News and filed Monday said. Florida is the first state to sue OpenAI and Altman over design and safety. “Last year’s mass shooting at Florida State University was a tragedy, but ChatGPT is not responsible for this terrible crime,” OpenAI spokesman Drew Pusateri said in a statement to NBC News after the company was sued by the family of a victim of the shooting.

items.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents - TechCrunch
items.summary: # Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman, in first-of-its-kind lawsuit over violent incidents. OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, were sued by the Florida Attorney General on Monday, in a first-of-its-kind state litigation effort over ChatGPT’s alleged links to a number of violent incidents. The lawsuit accuses OpenAI of looking the other way on safety concerns as it has sought to prioritize winning “the AI arms race and amass large fortunes.”. “Today, we announced the first-in-the-nation state-led lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman,” said Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier. That probe sought to determine what role ChatGPT may have played in a mass shooting that took place last year at Florida State University. OpenAI has also been sued in a civil suit by the family of one of the victims of that shooting.

items.title: OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness - MLQ.ai
items.summary: # OpenAI Launches Rosalind Biodefense, Offers Free AI Model to Governments for Pandemic Preparedness. * OpenAI launched Rosalind Biodefense, a two-track program giving vetted developers and U.S. government agencies free access to GPT-Rosalind for pandemic preparedness and biosecurity work [1]. * GPT-Rosalind outperforms GPT-5, GPT-5.2, and GPT-5.4 on internal benchmarks in chemistry, biochemistry, and experiment design [2]. OpenAI on May 29 launched Rosalind Biodefense, a program that provides free access to its specialized life sciences AI model, GPT-Rosalind, for government agencies, academic institutions, and nonprofit developers working on pandemic preparedness and biodefense [1]. The program operates on two tracks: a developer track sponsoring vetted teams building epidemiological models, early-detection tools, and screening systems, and a government track extending access to select U.S. federal agencies and allied partners for outbreak-response planning, diagnostics, and medical countermeasure development [3]. The Rosalind Biodefense program expands GPT-Rosalind's use from general life-science research into national security and public health applications, marking the first time OpenAI has offered a specialized model free of charge to government partners at this scale [3].
